compiled_state.title: Implementation planning gate does not catch newly-unblocked stories in sprint-next-batch.sh output
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Bug Description

When a story becomes unblocked mid-sprint (after its blocking story is closed in a batch), sprint-next-batch.sh includes it in the next batch as a dispatchable task. However, if the story has 0 children (no implementation tasks), it should not be dispatched directly — it needs implementation planning first.

## Root Cause

The Implementation Planning Gate (Phase 2 of /dso:sprint) only runs once during sprint initialization — it evaluates stories that are ready at that point. Stories that become ready later (unblocked by completed work) are never re-evaluated by the gate.

## Expected Behavior

sprint-next-batch.sh should either:
1. Detect stories with 0 children and exclude them from the batch (reporting them as SKIPPED_NEEDS_PLANNING), OR
2. The sprint orchestrator should re-run the Implementation Planning Gate check before each batch for any newly-unblocked stories

## Observed Behavior

Story cc36-54a7 (architect-foundation) was included in sprint-next-batch.sh output as a dispatchable task after its blocker (5b10-9f3b) was closed, despite having 0 implementation children.

## Impact

The orchestrator must manually detect this and create tasks before dispatching. Without the manual catch, a sub-agent would receive a story ticket (not a task) and attempt to implement the entire story in one shot — likely producing poor results.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: Stories with 0 children now emit SKIPPED_NEEDS_PLANNING instead of being dispatched as tasks
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
